# Figure description: скв_153.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/drill_holes_data/скв_153.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite borehole/well log** (комплексный геологический разрез скважины) — a Soviet-era well log sheet of the type standard in Soviet geological exploration practice. It combines a lithological column with multiple geophysical log curves plotted against depth, accompanied by a data table on the right side. This is likely a "паспорт скважины" (borehole passport) or a standard геолого-геофизический разрез (geological-geophysical section) for a single borehole.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a **single deep borehole**, with depth extending from the surface down to what appears to be approximately **1,000–1,200+ metres** based on the scale and proportional length of the log. The borehole number is not clearly legible at this resolution, but given the report context (Smolianova et al., 1983–1984, northern Kazakhstan, map sheets M-42 and N-42), it is likely one of the parametric or structural boreholes drilled as part of the regional survey. The log covers the full drilled interval and records lithological variation throughout.

The bottom section of the figure appears to show a **separate, shorter log segment** or a continuation panel, possibly representing an adjacent or shallower borehole, or a zoomed-in interval.

---

## Key Content

The figure is organised into several vertical tracks (columns), read left to right:

### Left Side — Lithological Column
- A **stratigraphic/lithological column** occupies the leftmost tracks. It uses standard Soviet lithological symbols (patterns of dots, dashes, brickwork, horizontal lines, etc.) to represent rock types. Visible patterns suggest alternating sequences of:
  - **Sandstones or siltstones** (dotted patterns)
  - **Clays or mudstones** (horizontal line patterns)
  - **Crystalline or igneous/metamorphic basement** (cross-hatch or irregular patterns) in deeper sections
  - **Carbonate rocks** (brick-like symbols) possibly present in middle sections
- Stratigraphic formation boundaries are marked with horizontal lines, with formation labels (partially illegible) along the left margin.
- A **depth scale** runs along the left edge, marked at regular intervals (likely every 10 or 20 metres).

### Central Tracks — Geophysical Log Curves
Two or three **geophysical curves** are plotted in the central portion of the figure against depth:

1. **A high-amplitude, high-frequency curve** (leftmost of the central curves) — this shows strong deflections throughout most of the section and is likely a **spontaneous potential (SP) log** or **resistivity log** (КС — кажущееся сопротивление). The curve shows:
   - High variability in the upper and lower sections
   - A notably **subdued, low-amplitude zone** in the middle portion of the log (roughly the middle third of the depth range), suggesting a relatively homogeneous or conductive interval (possibly a thick clay or evaporite sequence)
   - Return to high variability in the deeper section

2. **A second curve** (to the right of the first) — also showing variability but with somewhat different character, possibly a **gamma-ray log (ГК)** or **neutron log (НГК)**

3. A **third, less prominent curve** may be present near the centre, possibly a **caliper or microresistivity log**

### Right-Centre Track — Formation/Interval Descriptions
A narrow column contains **lithological descriptions and formation designations** (stratigraphic index symbols), partially legible. Standard Soviet stratigraphic indices (e.g., Є, O, S, D, C, P, T, J, K, etc.) may be present.

### Far Right — Data Table
A large **tabular data section** occupies the right third of the figure. It contains a grid with many columns and rows, likely recording:
- Core recovery percentages (выход керна, %)
- Drilling intervals (интервалы бурения)
- Rock descriptions per interval
- Sample/core numbers
- Possibly geochemical or mineralogical data
- Dates or drilling parameters

The table has a header row (partially legible at top) and continues for the full depth range. Individual cell values are **[illegible]** at this resolution.

### Bottom Panel
A **separate shorter log** appears below a horizontal dividing band near the bottom of the figure. This may represent:
- A **continuation** of the main borehole into deeper intervals
- A **different nearby borehole** logged on the same sheet for comparison
- A **repeat section** or interval of interest

This lower panel shows similar curve patterns with notable deflections.

This composite well log is the **primary subsurface data record** for the drilled borehole, providing a continuous lithological and petrophysical profile from surface to total depth. A geologist or AI research assistant would use
